item.title: No breach of FIFA rules in Ba player's pre-match ritual - Fiji FA
item.description: No breach of FIFA rules in Ba player's pre-match ritual - Fiji FA

item.title: Mbappe stars as France storms into World Cup last 16
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France cruised to a 3-0 victory over Sweden this morning to book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Mbappe's brace took his tournament tally to six goals, drawing level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot.
item.content:encoded:
Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France cruised to a 3-0 victory over Sweden this morning to book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

Mbappe's brace took his tournament tally to six goals, drawing level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot.

France will now face Paraguay in the Round of 16 after they stunned four-time world champions Germany on penalties.

The France and Paraguay clash will kick off at 9am on Sunday.

Norway has also progressed to the Round of 16 after edging Ivory Coast 2-1 this morning.

item.title: Doge called up to replace Tagi for Flying Fijians
item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua tighthead prop Mesake Doge has just been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship. Doge has been called up to replace Luke Tagi who is unavailable due to personal commitments.
item.content:encoded:
Swire Shipping Fijian Drua tighthead prop Mesake Doge has just been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship.

Doge has been called up to replace Luke Tagi who is unavailable due to personal commitments.

Fiji Rugby says they look forward to welcoming Tagi back into the squad in the future.

They congratulate Doge for his selection and wish him and the Flying Fijians squad all the success as they proudly represent Fiji.

The Flying Fijians take on Wales at 1.10am Sunday before facing England on July 12th at Hill Dickinson Stadium. They will then wrap up their tour against Scotland on July 19th at Murrayfield Stadium.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10.10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Halfpenny joins Wales coach team for summer Tests
item.description: Leigh Halfpenny has joined the Wales management team for the Nations Championship as kicking coach.The former full-back retired at

item.title: Norway beats Ivory Coast 2-1 to set up FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash with Brazil
item.description: Norway will face Brazil in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 on Monday after edging Ivory Coast 2-1 this morning. Norway levelled the match in the 74th minute before Erling Haaland sealed the victory with a goal in the 86th minute.
item.content:encoded:
Norway will face Brazil in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 on Monday after edging Ivory Coast 2-1 this morning.

Norway levelled the match in the 74th minute before Erling Haaland sealed the victory with a goal in the 86th minute.

The win books Norway's place in the last 16, where they will take on five-time world champions Brazil.

Meanwhile, France will face Sweden at 9 tonight.

item.title: Extra Premier League returns this Sunday with exciting clashes
item.description: The Extra Premier League resumes this Sunday with exciting clashes lined up.Defending champions Rewa will play Lautoka at 1pm at C

item.title: Morocco knocks out Netherlands on penalties to reach Round of 16
item.description: Morocco has booked their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 after defeating the Netherlands 3-2 in a penalty shootout following a 1-1 draw in their Round of 32 clash. The Netherlands looked set to book their place in the Round of 16 after Cody Gakpo broke the deadlock in the 72nd minute, with the goal set up by Crysencio Summerville .
item.content:encoded:
Morocco has booked their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 after defeating the Netherlands 3-2 in a penalty shootout following a 1-1 draw in their Round of 32 clash.

The Netherlands looked set to book their place in the Round of 16 after Cody Gakpo broke the deadlock in the 72nd minute, with the goal set up by Crysencio Summerville .

However, Morocco struck deep into added time when Ilias Diop found the equaliser in the 91st minute after an assist from Chemsdine Talbi , stunning the Dutch and forcing the match beyond regulation time.

In other matches today, Brazil booked their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 with a 2-1 victory over Japan, while Paraguay stunned Germany 4-3 in a penalty shootout after their clash ended 1-1 after extra time.

The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 continues tomorrow with Ivory Coast taking on Norway at 5am, France facing Sweden at 9am, and Mexico meeting Ecuador at 1pm.

item.description: Gabriel Martinelli struck deep into stoppage time to send Brazil into the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 with a dramatic 2-1 victory o
